compiled_state.title: As a DSO practitioner, deprecated sub-agent templates have consistent anti-cover-up guidance

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Update the deprecated fix-task-tdd.md and fix-task-mechanical.md templates to align their existing Anti-Patterns tables with the comprehensive five-pattern taxonomy established in SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md.
**Why**: Although deprecated, these templates may still be referenced by agents reading the prompts directory. Consistency prevents confusion if an agent encounters them.
**Scope**:
- IN: fix-task-tdd.md, fix-task-mechanical.md (update existing Anti-Patterns tables)
- OUT: SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md, task-execution.md — handled by Story 1. Review-time detection — handled by separate epics.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, fix-task-tdd.md Anti-Patterns table includes all five cover-up anti-patterns with code examples, rationale, and Do this instead alternatives
  <- Satisfies: Sub-agent prompt templates each include a Prohibited Fix Patterns section
- When this story is complete, fix-task-mechanical.md Anti-Patterns table includes the same five anti-patterns
  <- Satisfies: Sub-agent prompt templates each include a Prohibited Fix Patterns section
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] These templates are deprecated — changes should be minimal (extend existing table, not restructure)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Story complete: all 3 tasks closed, 22 tests GREEN
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
